arts.sum: The second name on Barca’s shortlist is considered ‘more realistic’ in Viktor Gyokeres, who has a release clause of around €100 million in his contract at Sporting Lisbon. However, Barca are aware that Lewandowski’s days are numbered due to his age and his status at one of the top earners at the club, combined with the final year of his contract only guaranteed if he plays 55% of the games this season.

arts.ttl: The 2 fixtures Barcelona are considering for Frenkie de Jong’s return
arts.sum: A fresh insight into the plans of the coaching staff at La Liga giants Barcelona to reintroduce Frenkie de Jong to competitive action has this weekend been provided. Dutch international…

After Friday's recovery session following the game against Monaco in midweek, Barça held their final workout of the week before heading south for their week 6 La Liga fixture against Villarreal on Sunday, kick off 6.30pm CEST. Furthermore, Hansi Flick's team will be aiming to bounce back after their first setback of the season in midweek, a 2-1 defeat away at Monaco in the Champions League.

That's FC Barcelona's aim for this LaLiga matchday six fixture as the squad travel to Valencia to take on Villarreal, kick-off 6:30pm CEST. Hansi Flick's side will be aiming to get the win to keep them at the top of the table and make it 18 points out of 18.

arts.ttl: PSG Was Ready to Make Huge Bid for Barcelona Sensation, Journalist Reveals
arts.sum: This summer, rumors swirled about Paris Saint-Germain eyeing Lamine Yamal as a potential successor to Kylian Mbappé. Recently, a journalist revealed that the French club was ready to offer €200M for him, but ultimately, the deal didn’t materialize. Mundo Deportivo reported over the summer that PSG was prepared to break its own transfer record, offering up […] The post PSG Was Ready to Make Huge Bid for Barcelona Sensation, Journalist Reveals appeared first on PSG Talk.

arts.ttl: Villarreal vs Barcelona: Preview, predictions and lineups
arts.sum: Barcelona are looking to bounce back from a disappointing Champions League defeat this Sunday when they travel to Villarreal in La Liga. However, Flick's side have won all five of their La Liga matches to date and are guaranteed to finish the weekend at the top of the table, regardless of results elsewhere or their own at Villarreal.

A difficult start to Barca's Champions League campaign - losing 2-1 to Monaco midweek - may have dented confidence levels slightly, but the Catalan giants will have ambitions of bouncing back at the Estadio de la Ceramica this weekend to maintain their lead at the summit of the table. Injuries and suspensions have made Flick's job significantly more challenging heading to Villarreal, with the likes of Dani Olmo, Gavi, Frenkie de Jong and Ronald Araujo all missing.
